signals: Entry-level coding seems easier to automate
Internship expectations appear higher than before
Students using AI tools widely, making differentiation harder

workflow_change.before: Learned by writing code directly and building projects step by step.
workflow_change.after: Uses AI for explanations, debugging help, and scaffolding, but is less sure how to demonstrate independent value.

actions: Focused more on problem understanding than only syntax
Built projects showing architecture and product thinking
Tried to learn when not to trust AI-generated code

outcome.early_effect: Learning feels faster, but standing out feels harder.

narrative.summary: AI helps students build faster, but it also raises the baseline. The challenge now feels less like writing code at all and more like showing judgment, understanding, and originality.
